references.text: Backer, CA & RC Bakhuizen van den Brink. 1963. Flora of Java 1: 315-318.

Majure LC, R Puente, MP Griffith, WS Judd, PS Soltis & DE Soltis. 2012. Phylogeny of Opuntia s.s. (Cactaceae): clade delineation, geographic origins, and reticulate evolution. American Journal of Botany 99: 847-864

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 October 2020

species.citation: (L.) Hummelinck , Succulenta (Netherlands) 20 (1938) 165; ≡ Cactus tetragonus L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 466. = Acanthocereus pentagonus (L.) Britton & Rose , Contr. U.S. Natl. Herb. 12 (1909) 432 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 129; ≡ Cactus pentagonus L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 467.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) Mill. , Gard. Dict., 8th Ed. (1768) n.1 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 129; ≡ Cactus hexagonus L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 466. = Cereus lepidotus Salm-Dyck , Cact. Hort. Dyck., 1849 Ed. (1850) 207 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 339.
species.notes: Occasionally planted for ornamental purposes, and in a few localities in regions subject to a long dry season, persistent in thickets, etc.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 January 2021

species.citation: (DC.) Haw. , Philos. Mag. Ann. Chem. 6 (1829) 109; ≡ Cactus oxypetalus Moc. & Sessé ex DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 3 (1828) 470.
species.notes: Possibly naturalized in forest in Tawi-Tawi (Kady Tahil, pers. comm., 22-Aug-2020).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser & Jayson Mansibang, 28 June 2022

species.citation: (Kunth) Lodé , Cact.-Avent. Int. 97 (2012) 26; ≡ Pereskia bleo (Kunth) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 3 (1828) 475; ≡ Cactus bleo Kunth , Nov. Gen. Sp. (H.B.K.), quarto Ed. 6 (1823) 69.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 July 2024

species.citation: (L.) Mill. , Gard. Dict., 8th Ed. (1768) n.6; ≡ Nopalea cochenillifera (L.) Salm-Dyck , Cact. Hort. Dyck., 1849 Ed. (1850) 64 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 340; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 129; ≡ Cactus cochenillifer L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 468.
species.notes: Planted here and there for ornamental purposes, in some regions subject to a long dry season. Naturalized. The cochineal cactus - dried bodies of mealy bugs yields carmine, a scarlet dye.

species.citation: (Willd.) Haw. , Suppl. Pl. Succ. (1819) 81 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 130; ≡ Cactus monacanthos Willd. , Enum. Pl. Suppl. (Willdenow) (1814) 33.
species.notes: Now more or less distributed in the Old World tropics. Sandy soil near the beach. Invasive species (CABI 2017).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser & Jayson Mansibang, 28 June 2022
Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 2 September 2022

species.citation: (Haw.) D.R.Hunt , Cactaceae Syst. Init. 36 (2017) 35; ≡ Hylocereus undatus (Haw.) Britton & Rose , Fl. Bermuda (1918) 256 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 129; ≡ Cereus undatus Haw. , Philos. Mag. Ann. Chem. 9 (1830) 110. ≠ Cereus triangularis auct. non Haw. nec Mill. ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 99; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 339.
species.notes: Cultivated very extensively in some provinces for its fruit and ornamental purposes. Not spontaneous. Dragon fruit, pitaya.
